description_long: This is a four-story apartment building with a steep, reddish-brown gabled roof and a prominent brick chimney on one side. The front facade features a half-timbered design with exposed wooden beams and beige plaster, giving it a traditional European appearance. The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns, with two dormer windows set into the roof and larger rectangular windows on each floor. The rear and side walls are plain, finished in a smooth beige plaster, with minimal architectural detailing except for the roof windows. The building's entrance is located at the base of the front facade, beneath the timber framing. The overall structure suggests a sturdy, masonry construction typical of older urban residential buildings.
description: A four-story, half-timbered apartment building with a steep gabled roof, brick chimney, and symmetrical window arrangement. The front features exposed wooden beams and a traditional European style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the half-timbered facade, two dormer windows, and the main entrance at the base.
description_view_1: Rear view with plain beige plaster wall, two roof windows, and the brick chimney visible on the left.
description_view_2: Side view with symmetrical window columns, a single dormer window, and the chimney on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with plain plaster wall, two roof windows, and the chimney on the right edge.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on the front and side facades, with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors. Dormer windows are set into the roof, and all windows are rectangular.
